title: Callerya longipedunculata
taxonomy: Callerya longipedunculata is classified within the family Fabaceae, commonly known as legumes. It belongs to the subfamily Faboideae and tribe Sophoreae. This plant is part of the genus Callerya, which is primarily native to Asia. Callerya longipedunculata is a climbing or twining vine that is often found in tropical regions. The specific classification of Callerya longipedunculata provides insight into its evolutionary relationships and botanical characteristics.

intro: Callerya longipedunculata, also known as the "dual fruit," is a type of legume that belongs to the Fabaceae family. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in treating various ailments such as fever, cough, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Callerya longipedunculata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Callerya longipedunculata is commonly prepared into teas, infusions, or decoctions to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
